project.name: cc-nerf-buster
project.tagline: Directly measuring the size of Claude Code's quota — what 100% actually is, in tokens, with bracketed precision.

hero.code:
# Measured 2026-04-24 against a Claude Max account
# Quota expressed in Opus cache-write tokens

  Window     Full quota (mid)    per 1% tick
  ─────────────────────────────────────────
  5-hour     16,895,532          168,955
  7-day      85,846,742          858,467

# Spread between low/high bounds: under 0.3%
# 7-day quota ≈ 5.08× the 5-hour quota
#
# The internal quota is a weighted budget across input,
# output, cache-read, and cache-write tokens — each
# priced differently. Cache-writes above are one
# convenient projection of that weighted budget.

highlights.title: What 100% actually is
highlights.description: Claude Code shows you a percentage but never tells you what the denominator is. cc-nerf-buster pins the denominator to a concrete number, in the token kind of your choice, with measurement bands.

highlights.title: Two windows, separately probed
highlights.description: The 5-hour and 7-day rolling budgets aren't proportional — each gets its own utilization gauge and its own measurement run. Both numbers are reported independently.

highlights.title: Weighted-cost methodology
highlights.description: Each request is converted into a weighted cost using published API pricing ratios: model tier × token-kind multiplier, summed across the mix. Only the ratios matter, not absolute prices.

highlights.title: Bracketed precision
highlights.description: The utilization meter only ticks in whole percent. Each tick is bracketed by the last observation before the crossing and the first after, yielding low / midpoint / high bounds — measured spread is under 0.3%.

highlights.title: Local proxy intercepts the truth
highlights.description: A local Go proxy sits between Claude Code and api.anthropic.com, parsing every request/response pair: input, output, cache-read, cache-write tokens, plus model used. No log scraping, no estimation.

highlights.title: Reproducible on your own account
highlights.description: One command — just probe — drives Claude Code in a loop, watches the gauge tick, and prints the bounds. Your quota size, your numbers, your account.

quickstart.code:
# Install
git clone https://github.com/brandon-fryslie/cc-nerf-buster
cd cc-nerf-buster
just install

# Run a full probe (both 5h and 7d windows)
just probe

# Or one window at a time
just probe-5h
just probe-7d

# Resume an interrupted run
just probe --continue

# Output: 5h and 7d low/mid/high bounds, plus per-1%-tick
# token projections for the model you pinned.
